methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Ark Robotics is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Achi Takagama. Valuation analysis relies on a peer-median approach applied to a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Stark Defence at $500M Series A, Vatn Systems at $300M Series A, Ares Industries at $150M Series A, and Tenna Systems at $60M Seed), alongside reference to a broader comp set of 2 public companies and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

The peer-median method yields a central implied valuation of $225M, derived from the median of the four identified comparables, which range from $60M to $500M. This single-method triangulation produces a low estimate of $191M and a high estimate of $259M, reflecting a ±15% sensitivity band around the median.

The $191M–$259M range is supported primarily by peer positioning, though the analysis carries material uncertainty. The seed stage of Ark Robotics sits below three Series A comparables, which typically command valuation premiums; conversely, Tenna Systems at $60M seed-stage provides a lower boundary but may reflect different technology maturity or market conditions. Stage-appropriate adjustments and additional valuation methods would strengthen confidence; current data supports a median estimate of $225M with acknowledged reliance on a limited methodological foundation.
